---
title: "Heavy/Medium-Duty Truck Supercharging Station Feasibility Analysis"
subtitle: "Guangdong Dongguan · Changping Railway Station Hub"
author: "Feasibility Study Group"
date: today
lang: en
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
  typst:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    page-margin: [2.2cm, 2.2cm, 2.2cm, 2.2cm]
    mainfont: "Noto Sans CJK SC"
    mainfontfallback: ["Microsoft YaHei"]
    fontsize: 10.5pt
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Instructions

Drag the sliders on the left to adjust parameters. All text, metrics, tables, and charts below will **recalculate in real time**.
Once parameters are finalized, click the "Export Parameters" button at the bottom to copy the JSON and save it as `params.json`.

```bash
python convert.py feasibility.qmd --params params.json
quarto render feasibility-static.qmd --to typst
```

This will produce a professionally typeset PDF.

```{python}
#| label: setup
import json
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib as mpl
from shiny import reactive
from shiny.express import input, render, ui
import model as mlib

mpl.rcParams["font.sans-serif"] = ["Noto Sans CJK SC", "Microsoft YaHei", "SimHei"]
mpl.rcParams["axes.unicode_minus"] = False
mpl.rcParams["figure.dpi"] = 110
```

```{python}
#| label: params-defaults
# Default parameters — also defines the key set for converter-generated params.yml
DEFAULTS = {
    "n_heavy": 12, "power_heavy": 480, "daily_sessions_heavy": 6,
    "kwh_per_session_heavy": 280, "capex_heavy_per_charger": 350000,
    "n_medium": 15, "power_medium": 150, "daily_sessions_medium": 8,
    "kwh_per_session_medium": 100, "capex_medium_per_charger": 80000,
    "service_fee": 0.45, "electricity_sell": 0.72, "electricity_purchase": 0.65,
    "capex_civil_kw": 900, "land_rent_year": 1200000,
    "staff_cost_year": 720000, "other_opex_year": 240000,
    "maintenance_rate": 0.03, "discount_rate": 0.08, "years": 10,
}
```

```{python}
#| label: model-reactive
@reactive.calc
def model():
    p = {
        "n_heavy": input.n_heavy(),
        "power_heavy": input.power_heavy(),
        "daily_sessions_heavy": input.dsh(),
        "kwh_per_session_heavy": input.ksh(),
        "capex_heavy_per_charger": input.caph() * 1e4,
        "n_medium": input.n_medium(),
        "daily_sessions_medium": input.dsm(),
        "kwh_per_session_medium": input.ksm(),
        "capex_medium_per_charger": input.capm() * 1e4,
        "service_fee": input.service_fee(),
        "electricity_sell": input.esell(),
        "electricity_purchase": input.epurchase(),
        "capex_civil_kw": input.civil_kw(),
        "land_rent_year": input.land() * 1e4,
        "discount_rate": input.dr(),
        "years": int(input.years()),
    }
    return mlib.compute(p)
```

## Parameter Panel

```{python}
#| label: ui-sidebar
with ui.sidebar():
    ui.h4("Heavy-duty Supercharging")
    ui.input_slider("n_heavy", "Heavy-duty berths", 4, 24, 12, step=1)
    ui.input_slider("power_heavy", "Per-unit power (kW)", 300, 600, 480, step=10)
    ui.input_slider("dsh", "Daily sessions per charger", 2, 12, 6, step=1)
    ui.input_slider("ksh", "Energy per session (kWh)", 150, 350, 280, step=10)
    ui.input_slider("caph", "Equipment cost per charger (10k CNY)", 20, 50, 35, step=1)

    ui.h4("Medium-duty Fast Charging")
    ui.input_slider("n_medium", "Medium-duty berths", 5, 25, 15, step=1)
    ui.input_slider("dsm", "Daily sessions per charger", 3, 14, 8, step=1)
    ui.input_slider("ksm", "Energy per session (kWh)", 50, 160, 100, step=5)
    ui.input_slider("capm", "Equipment cost per charger (10k CNY)", 4, 15, 8, step=1)

    ui.h4("Tariffs & Electricity Price (CNY/kWh)")
    ui.input_slider("service_fee", "Service fee", 0.20, 0.70, 0.45, step=0.01)
    ui.input_slider("esell", "Selling price (to user)", 0.55, 0.90, 0.72, step=0.01)
    ui.input_slider("epurchase", "Purchase price (from grid)", 0.45, 0.80, 0.65, step=0.01)

    ui.h4("Investment & Operations")
    ui.input_slider("civil_kw", "Power expansion (CNY/kW)", 500, 1500, 900, step=50)
    ui.input_slider("land", "Annual land rent (10k CNY)", 40, 250, 120, step=5)
    ui.input_slider("demand", "Demand achievement multiplier", 0.5, 1.3, 1.0, step=0.05)

    ui.h4("Financial Assumptions")
    ui.input_slider("dr", "Discount rate", 0.05, 0.15, 0.08, step=0.005)
    ui.input_slider("years", "Project life (years)", 6, 15, 10, step=1)
```

# Project Overview

```{python}
#| label: text-overview
@render.ui
def text_overview():
    m = model()
    return ui.markdown(f"""This project proposes to build **{m['site_name']}** at **{m['location']}**.
The site measures **100 m × 57 m (≈ {m['site_area_m2']/1e4:.2f} 10k m²)**, planned with
**{m['n_heavy']} heavy-duty supercharging berths** ({m['power_heavy']} kW liquid-cooled superchargers) and
**{m['n_medium']} medium-duty charging berths** ({m['power_medium']} kW dual-gun fast chargers),
totaling **{m['n_heavy'] + m['n_medium']} truck charging berths**. The site is adjacent to the Changping Railway Station rail-logistics node,
providing fast charging services for transit heavy-duty trucks and urban-distribution medium-duty trucks.
""")
```

# Location & Market Analysis

```{python}
#| label: text-location
@render.ui
def text_location():
    m = model()
    return ui.markdown(f"""- **Location conditions**: Changping Town is situated in eastern Dongguan, where the Beijing–Kowloon Railway
  and the Guangzhou–Shenzhen Railway intersect. The Changping Railway Station and surrounding freight yards
  serve as an important regional rail-to-road logistics transfer node. The project site is approximately 300 m
  east of the railway station, adjacent to arterial roads, with convenient heavy-truck access. The surrounding
  area has dense clusters of manufacturing parks and logistics warehouses, with ample traffic from both
  long-haul heavy-duty trucks and urban-distribution medium-duty trucks.
- **Site suitability**: The {m['site_area_m2']/1e4:.2f} 10k m² site can accommodate {m['n_heavy'] + m['n_medium']} truck berths
  (each berth, including turning radius, requires ~{m['berth_area']} m², totaling ~{m['berth_total_area']/1e4:.2f} 10k m²),
  along with the transformer/substation building, rest/office areas, and driving lanes — the layout is feasible.
- **Demand assessment**: Heavy-duty trucks require large single-session charging volumes and are sensitive to
  supercharging power, making them well-suited for {m['power_heavy']} kW liquid-cooled superchargers. Medium-duty trucks
  primarily serve short-haul urban distribution with smaller per-session energy needs, making them suitable for
  {m['power_medium']} kW-class dual-gun fast chargers. The overlap of these two customer segments at a logistics node
  helps improve charger utilization across both daytime and nighttime periods.
- **Key constraints**: Total station connected load is approximately **{m['connected_kw']/1e3:,.0f} MW (≈ {m['connected_kw']:,.0f} kW)**,
  requiring a 10 kV dedicated power supply and package substation. The approval and construction timeline
  for the external line is the critical path for project delivery.
""")
```

# CAPEX Estimate

```{python}
#| label: text-capex-intro
@render.ui
def text_capex_intro():
    m = model()
    return ui.markdown(f"""CAPEX consists of charging equipment and accessories (split between heavy-duty and medium-duty),
civil works, grid connection, and power capacity expansion. Power expansion is estimated at
{m['capex_civil_kw']} CNY/kW of total connected load. (Heavy-duty per-unit equipment: {m['capex_heavy_per_charger']/1e4:.0f} 10k CNY;
medium-duty per-unit equipment: {m['capex_medium_per_charger']/1e4:.0f} 10k CNY.)
""")
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-capex
@render.data_frame
def tbl_capex():
    m = model()
    df = mlib.capex_table(m)
    df["Amount (CNY)"] = df["Amount (CNY)"].map(lambda x: f"{x:,.0f}")
    df["Share"] = df["Share"].map(lambda x: f"{x*100:.1f}%")
    return df
```

# Revenue & Cost Projections

```{python}
#| label: text-proforma-intro
@render.ui
def text_proforma_intro():
    m = model()
    unit_price = m['service_fee'] + m['electricity_sell']
    margin_per_kwh = m['margin']
    return ui.markdown(f"""Annual energy is estimated as "number of chargers × daily sessions per charger × energy per session × 365",
with a ramp-up coefficient applied to charging sessions during the cultivation period. Revenue consists of
**service fee** ({m['service_fee']:.2f} CNY/kWh) and **electricity sales** ({m['electricity_sell']:.2f} CNY/kWh);
the total price to the user is approximately **{unit_price:.2f}** CNY/kWh.
Costs include electricity purchased from the grid ({m['electricity_purchase']:.2f} CNY/kWh),
land rent ({m['land_rent_year']/1e4:.0f} 10k CNY/year), staffing ({m['staff_cost_year']/1e4:.0f} 10k CNY/year),
other operating expenses ({m['other_opex_year']/1e4:.0f} 10k CNY/year), and maintenance
accrued at {m['maintenance_rate']*100:.0f}% of CAPEX.

> Gross margin per kWh = service fee + (selling price − purchase price) = {margin_per_kwh:.2f} CNY/kWh.
> Under a pure pass-through electricity model (selling price = purchase price), the project relies solely on service fee revenue.
""")
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-proforma
@render.data_frame
def tbl_proforma():
    m = model()
    df = mlib.proforma_table(m)
    money = ["Revenue (10k CNY)", "Power cost (10k CNY)", "Opex (10k CNY)", "EBITDA (10k CNY)", "Pre-tax profit (10k CNY)"]
    for c in money:
        df[c] = df[c].map(lambda x: f"{x:,.0f}")
    for c in ["Heavy (10k kWh)", "Medium (10k kWh)", "Total (10k kWh)"]:
        df[c] = df[c].map(lambda x: f"{x:,.1f}")
    return df
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-revenue
@render.plot(alt="Annual energy and EBITDA")
def fig_revenue():
    return mlib.plot_revenue(model())
```

# Financial Evaluation

```{python}
#| label: text-finance-intro
@render.ui
def text_finance_intro():
    m = model()
    return ui.markdown(f"""With a one-time CAPEX investment of {m['capex']/1e4:,.0f} 10k CNY as the year-0 cash outflow,
and each operating year's EBITDA as cash inflow, plus residual asset recovery at the end of the project life at
{m['residual_rate']*100:.0f}% of CAPEX ({m['capex']*m['residual_rate']/1e4:,.0f} 10k CNY),
the net present value is computed at a discount rate of {m['discount_rate']*100:.0f}%.
""")
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-metrics
@render.data_frame
def tbl_metrics():
    return mlib.metrics_table(model())
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-cashflow
@render.plot(alt="Cumulative discounted cash flow and payback period")
def fig_cashflow():
    return mlib.plot_cashflow(model())
```

# Sensitivity Analysis

```{python}
#| label: text-sensitivity-intro
@render.ui
def text_sensitivity_intro():
    return ui.markdown(f"""Examines the impact on NPV of two key variables: **demand achievement rate** and
**service fee unit price** (using steady-state mature-year calculations; all other parameters
held at defaults). Red regions in the heatmap indicate negative NPV, where the project is not viable.
""")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-sensitivity
@render.plot(alt="NPV sensitivity heatmap")
def fig_sensitivity():
    return mlib.plot_sensitivity(model())
```

# Risks & Uncertainties

```{python}
#| label: text-risks
@render.ui
def text_risks():
    m = model()
    ramp_mid = m['demand_ramp'][len(m['demand_ramp'])//2] * 100
    return ui.markdown(f"""- **Demand risk**: Actual charging sessions are influenced by logistics sector sentiment,
  competing station layouts in the vicinity, and trunk-line traffic volumes. If the long-term achievement rate
  stays below **{ramp_mid:.0f}%**, the project payback period will be substantially lengthened.
- **Grid connection risk**: The station's total connected load of ~{m['connected_kw']/1e3:.0f} MW requires a
  10 kV dedicated line and package substation. Uncertainty around external line approval, construction
  duration, and expansion costs is the largest variable affecting both CAPEX and project schedule.
  Smart load management (sequential charging, battery storage peak shaving) can reduce the declared
  capacity requirement and expansion investment.
- **Electricity pricing policy risk**: Guangdong commercial and industrial electricity pricing follows
  time-of-use (ToU) tariffs with significant peak–off-peak spreads. Service fees are subject to local
  regulatory price caps; heavy/medium-duty truck charging rates must comply with the latest local regulations.
- **Technology iteration**: The proliferation of megawatt-class supercharging (MCS) and liquid-cooling
  technology may shorten the economic life of existing {m['power_heavy']} kW equipment, affecting residual
  value and replacement cycle assumptions.
- **Site risk**: The stability of the {m['site_area_m2']/1e4:.2f} 10k m² site lease and rising land prices
  will directly affect fixed operating costs.
""")
```

# Conclusion

```{python}
#| label: text-conclusion
@render.ui
def text_conclusion():
    m = model()
    verd = "Financially viable" if m['verdict'] == 'Financially viable' else "Financially infeasible ⚠️"
    return ui.markdown(f"""Under default parameters, **{m['site_name']}** ({m['n_heavy']} heavy-duty superchargers +
{m['n_medium']} medium-duty fast chargers) has a total investment of **{m['capex']/1e4:,.0f} 10k CNY**,
a {m['years']}-year net present value of **NPV = {m['npv']/1e4:,.0f} 10k CNY**,
an internal rate of return of **IRR = {m['irr']*100:.1f}%**, and a static payback period of
approximately **{m['payback']:.1f} years**.

Overall assessment: the project is **{verd}**. The key preconditions are timely grid connection
delivery and realization of logistics node demand. It is recommended that, prior to contract signing,
the grid connection plan and electricity price/service fee assumptions be confirmed using the
critical threshold combinations from the sensitivity analysis where NPV turns positive, and that
the hedging effect of battery storage + sequential charging on expansion investment be evaluated.
""")
```

# Export Current Parameters

```{python}
#| label: export-params
@render.ui
def export_params():
    m = model()
    export = {k: m[k] for k in DEFAULTS if k in m}
    j = json.dumps(export, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    return ui.card(
        ui.card_header("Current Parameters (JSON)"),
        ui.tags.pre(j),
    )
```
